---
title: "Interim Analysis"
author: "Ciaran Evans"
output: beamer_presentation
---

## Motivation: Peaking at the data?

Observe data $X_1,...,X_n$ over $n$ months, with single $X_i$ collected each month. Assume that
$$X_1,...,X_n \overset{iid}{\sim} N(\mu, 1)$$
Want to test $H_0: \mu = 0$ vs. $H_A: \mu > 0$. Initial plan: reject if 
$$Z_n = \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \sum \limits_{i=1}^n X_i > z_{\alpha}$$
**Question:** Can we look at the data early?

\vspace{4cm}

## Motivation: Peaking at the data?

$$X_1,...,X_n \overset{iid}{\sim} N(\mu, 1)$$
$$Z_k = \frac{1}{\sqrt{k}} \sum \limits_{i=1}^k X_i \hspace{1cm} Z_n = \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \sum \limits_{i=1}^n X_i$$
**Decision rule:** Reject $H_0$ if $Z_k > c_1$ or $Z_n > c_2$.

**Question:** How would I choose $c_1, c_2$ to control type I error at level $\alpha$?

\vspace{5cm}
